notes: v0.3 adds linear growth (fσ8) as a first-class observable without altering Phase-2 kernel equations.
v0.3.1 adds radial collapse solver (hole sector) reusing the same operator stack.
v0.3.2 adds Phase III: barrier dominance order parameter, constrained endpoint law, information ledger skeleton, exterior falsifier program.
v0.3.3 freezes WP2 (ringdown/echo) as candidate falsifier channel. WP2A-D complete; further progress requires interior PDE.

v0.3.4 revises WP2 freeze: PDE closure (Phase III-C) reclassifies interior from reactive_candidate (Q~515) to mixed_viscoelastic (Q~6-7.5). Proxy result superseded. Structural identity omega_0*tau=1 documented. WP3 null-result confirmation added.

v0.3.5 adds covariant interior closure: effective metric ansatz CONFIRMS PDE results. omega_0*tau=1 PRESERVED, Q~6 PRESERVED, mixed_viscoelastic PRESERVED. Reflection modified ±20% by metric corrections. Echo channel surviving at ~1.1%. 5 closures resolved, 7 remaining.

v0.3.6 adds covariant field equation framework (Phase III Final). Three candidate formulations evaluated: algebraic tensor (INSUFFICIENT), auxiliary scalar field (PREFERRED, scalarized first pass), nonlocal kernel (formal parent). Weak/strong-field reductions recover current solver at effective level. Bianchi compatibility at effective level. 7 closures remain. T^Phi_mu_nu is schematic/effective. Scalar is minimal closure; tensorial generalization open.

v1.0 release: Canonical Build (Phases I-III Complete). All classification strings synchronized (reactive_candidate -> reactive for Q>10). Default Q fallback updated to PDE value (7.5). Packages A (memory tensor), B (junction conditions), C (observables) added. 25 nonclaims. Status ladder applied consistently. Superseded results explicitly marked.

CONST_LAMBDA_LOCK.description: Geometric anchor approx 2/sqrt(3). Fundamental scaling ratio.

CONST_TAU_0.description: Canonical relaxation time derived in Phase 1.

PARAM_ALPHA_MEM.description: Weight of memory-smoothed driver in H^2 correction. 0 disables memory correction.

PARAM_C_RHO.description: Maps rho to H^2 in code units (placeholder until GRUT normalization is locked).

PARAM_C_K.description: Curvature coupling coefficient in code units (placeholder).

PARAM_W_EOS.description: Equation of state parameter p = w rho for baseline fluid evolution.

PARAM_SIGMA8_0.description: Linear growth normalization at z=0 (baseline σ8).

PARAM_Z_START.description: Default starting redshift when start_z is not provided in run_config.

NUM_H_CAP.description: Numerical cap for |H| used by stiffening guardrail.

NUM_GAMMA_H.description: Positive-definite damping rate applied to H each step.

state_vector.name: M_X
state_vector.description: Memory state for driver X (ODE equivalent)

memory_kernel.kernel_definition: K(s) = (1/τ_eff) exp(-s/τ_eff) Θ(s), with ∫_0^∞ K(s) ds = 1

math: Sanitize inputs (a>=a_min, rho>=0, p=w*rho unless provided)
Recompute H(t0) using GRUT closure with M_X(t0)
set p := w * rho (baseline EOS unless overridden by caller)
If |H| > H_cap then H := sign(H) * H_cap and log warning L_STIFF_ACTIVATED
Apply per-step damping: H := H * exp(-γ_H Δt)
Define τ_eff(H) using τ0 and dimensionless (H τ0)
Integrate D and D' in ln a: D'' + [2 + dlnH/dlna] D' - 3/2 Ωm(a) D = 0
Compute t_dp and t_grut under selected ω policy
Optional scan over ω or m (context-driven)

OP_RADIAL_COLLAPSE.role: Spherically symmetric dust collapse with memory coupling
math: dV/dt = -a_net;  a_net = a_inward - a_outward;  a_inward = (1-alpha)*GM/R^2 + alpha*M_drive;  a_outward = a_Q
Post-step L_stiff: if |V/R| > H_cap then V = -H_cap*R
derived_quantities: r_sat (CANDIDATE under OP_QPRESS_001: R_eq/r_s = epsilon_Q^(1/beta_Q). Without barrier: L_stiff artifact.)
collapse_class: stall | arrested_prehorizon | arrested_posthorizon | plunging | singular
tau0/t_dyn: timescale competition ratio (key hole-sector control parameter)

sign_theorem.tier_1: M <= ~10^24 kg: sign-definite (a_net > 0 always, M_drive > 0 always). STRUCTURAL.
sign_theorem.tier_2: M >= ~10^30 kg: M_drive and a_net can go transiently negative. V <= 0 holds numerically. CONDITIONAL.
sign_theorem.note: The originally claimed universal sign-definiteness does NOT survive into the astrophysical regime.

equations.name: LCDM reference expansion
equations.math: E_LCDM(z) = sqrt( Omega_m*(1+z)^3 + Omega_k*(1+z)^2 + Omega_Lambda + Omega_r*(1+z)^4 )

equations.name: Collapse net acceleration (canonical decomposition)
equations.math: a_inward = (1-alpha)*GM/R^2 + alpha*M_drive;  a_outward = a_Q (OP_QPRESS_001);  a_net = a_inward - a_outward

equations.name: Bounce exclusion (two-tier)
equations.math: Tier 1 (M <= ~10^24 kg): a_net > 0, M_drive > 0 always — sign-definite. Tier 2 (M >= ~10^30 kg): V <= 0 holds numerically despite transient a_net < 0 — conditional.

equations.name: Timescale competition law
equations.math: tau0/t_dyn = tau0 / ((pi/2)*sqrt(R0^3/(2GM))) controls collapse regime: >>1 freezes, ~1 participates, <<1 approaches GR

deterministic_run.description: No randomization; stable hashing of inputs/outputs.

baseline_eos_p_equals_w_rho.description: Use p=w rho with PARAM_W_EOS unless overridden.

observables.OBS_SLOPE: predicted log-log slope in mass scan

collapse.analogy: Oppenheimer-Snyder interior = closed FRW; same operator stack governs both

preferred_constrained_law.note: Preferred constrained law. NOT a fully first-principles-derived Tier A result. Micro-derivation of alpha_vac^2 coupling remains open.

epsilon_Q.description: Quantum pressure coupling. 0=off, >0 activates barrier. Preferred constrained value: alpha_vac^2 = 1/9.

beta_Q.description: Barrier steepness exponent. Constrained by Kretschner curvature scaling: K ∝ C^2 gives beta_Q = 2.

properties.mass_independent: R_eq/r_s does not depend on M
properties.asymptotically_stable: Restoring force on both sides of R_eq (d(a_net)/dR > 0)
properties.endpoint_motion: EMPIRICAL — classified by runs as sign_definite_infall, equilibrium_restoring, overshoot_damped, or bounce_violation. NOT assumed sign-definite.

quantum_pressure_barrier.note: Candidate endpoint operator. Default OFF (epsilon_Q=0). Preferred constrained values (epsilon_Q=1/9, beta_Q=2) pass the full anti-artifact acceptance suite. Micro-derivation required for promotion to fully derived.

missing_closures: Curvature-to-force mapping from vacuum stiffness gradient
Covariant extension of Newtonian-gauge power law

bandwidth_saturation.concept: Vacuum susceptibility gradient creates effective pressure
bandwidth_saturation.note: Compatible with curvature track but does not independently determine epsilon_Q or beta_Q

anomaly_bridge.note: Deferred to Phase III-B or later

r_sat.description: Saturation radius where collapse halts at force balance. Falls out of ODE integration where a_net -> 0 under quantum pressure barrier.
r_sat.constrained_law: r_sat = alpha_vac * r_s = (1/3) r_s (under preferred constrained values epsilon_Q = 1/9, beta_Q = 2)
r_sat.note: Physical endpoint requires OP_QPRESS_001. Without barrier (epsilon_Q = 0): r_sat is UNDEMONSTRATED (L_stiff artifact). With constrained law: r_sat = (1/3) r_s passes all 11 anti-artifact acceptance criteria.

t_dyn.description: Free-fall time. The hole-sector dynamical timescale.

tau0_over_t_dyn.description: Key hole-sector control parameter. Governs whether memory participates in collapse: >>1 freezes, ~1 participates, <<1 approaches GR.

collapse_class.description: Physics-level classification of collapse outcome, independent of integration termination.

bounce_exclusion_tier.description: Tier 1: a_net > 0, M_drive > 0 throughout. Tier 2: V <= 0 but signs not definite. Tier 3: bounce detected.

barrier_dominance.description: Primary order parameter for the Quantum Fluid to Barrier-Dominated Compact Core transition. Phi=0: fluid regime; Phi~0.5: crystallization threshold; Phi->1: force-balanced core.
barrier_dominance.note: LOCKED in current solver language. Phi is computed from canonical force decomposition and recorded at endpoint as barrier_dominance_final.

phase_III_B_mapping.note: Mapping from benchmark run M=1e30 kg, constrained law. Transition characteristics for other mass ranges not yet tested.

crystallization_threshold.status: CANDIDATE — benchmark places this at R/r_s ≈ 0.47, post-horizon

barrier_dominated_compact_core.status: CANDIDATE — reached at R_eq under constrained law

whole_hole.status: ACTIVE / RESEARCH TARGET — exterior observables not yet computed

variables.I_fields: Bekenstein-Hawking-like area proxy: pi R^2 / l_P^2 (CLASSICAL, not quantum)
variables.conservation_domain: UNDEFINED — not yet specified as local or global
variables.conservation_status: UNTESTED — no dynamic conservation check implemented

nonclaims: Does NOT prove information conservation
Does NOT solve the black hole information paradox
Does NOT derive unitarity from GRUT structure
All quantities are PROXY definitions, not fundamental measures

exterior_falsifier_program.description: Exterior observable predictions from the candidate interior endpoint at R_eq = (1/3) r_s. WP1 (exterior matching) and WP2 (ringdown echo) complete; WP3 (shadow/accretion null-result) confirmed. Covariant interior closure CONFIRMS PDE structural results.

WP1_exterior_matching.status: ANALYSIS COMPLETE — conditional GO for WP2/WP3
WP1_exterior_matching.question: What exterior metric or effective matching behavior is implied by a barrier-dominated compact core at 1/3 r_s?

result.confidence: moderate — consistent with solver, not proven
conditions: M_drive is matter-local (not a spacetime field)
OP_QPRESS_001 has no exterior gravitational self-energy
result.wp2_decision: CONDITIONAL GO — interior echoes can be computed under Schwarzschild background
result.wp3_decision: CONDITIONAL GO — expected null result under Schwarzschild exterior
result.missing_closure: Covariant treatment required for rigorous determination

WP2_ringdown_echo.status: FROZEN CANDIDATE FALSIFIER CHANNEL — PDE-informed constrained estimate (mixed_viscoelastic, Q~6-7.5, echo ~1.1%)
WP2_ringdown_echo.question: Could inward-propagating perturbations interact with the core/transition region to generate echoes or modified ringdown?

result.channel_assessment: CANDIDATE FALSIFIER CHANNEL — weakened but surviving under PDE closure; negligible under Boltzmann model
result.confidence: order_of_magnitude — Schwarzschild tortoise coordinate used inside horizon

impedance_model.status: SUPERSEDED BY PDE — sharp-boundary approximation retained as baseline comparison
impedance_model.formula_implemented: eta = omega_core * R_eq / c; r_surface_amp = |1 - eta| / (1 + eta)
impedance_model.pde_note: PDE closure corrects omega_core (proxy had extra 1/R_eq factor). With PDE-corrected sound speed c_s = omega_eff * R_eq, r_PDE ≈ 0.30 replaces r_proxy ≈ 0.98 as leading estimate
impedance_model.mass_scaling: eta decreases with mass (approximately M^{-1/2} confirmed numerically)
impedance_model.note_amplitude_vs_power: r_surface_amp is AMPLITUDE coefficient; power = r_amp^2
impedance_model.sharp_boundary_caveat: Assumes sharp transition at R_eq; Phase III-B transition has finite width; transition-width corrections remain open
nonclaims: Physically motivated estimate, not rigorous derivation
Actual r_surface_amp requires interior wave equation on GRUT metric
Boltzmann model (r_amp ≈ 0) remains viable if BDCC is dissipative
Mass scaling is consequence of impedance model, not universal
Pre-PDE r_amp ~ 0.98 is SUPERSEDED — do not use as leading estimate

interior_wave_model.status: SUPERSEDED BY PDE CLOSURE — historical proxy retained as baseline
interior_wave_model.approach: Damped harmonic oscillator with storage (stability eigenvalue) and loss (memory-mediated damping) channels
interior_wave_model.superseded_note: This proxy used omega_core^2 = beta_Q*GM/R_eq^4 (extra 1/R_eq factor). The PDE-correct eigenfrequency is omega_0^2 = beta_Q*GM/R_eq^3. The proxy underestimated omega*tau by a factor of sqrt(R_eq) ~ 172 at 30 M_sun, placing the system in the low-damping regime instead of the correct peak-damping regime.
interior_wave_model.quality_factor_Q: PROXY: Q = 515 (reactive). SUPERSEDED by PDE: Q = 6-7.5 (mixed_viscoelastic)
interior_wave_model.loss_tangent: PROXY: tan(delta) ≈ 0.001. SUPERSEDED by PDE: tan(delta) ≈ 0.07-0.08

viscoelastic_classification.mixed_viscoelastic: 1 < Q < 10, both channels active, band-limited echoes (CURRENT PDE RESULT)

interior_wave_model.dominant_damping: memory-mediated (from tau_eff coupling), not solver gamma_diss

proxy_result_30_Msun.status: SUPERSEDED — eigenfrequency error identified by PDE closure

nonclaims: Does NOT solve wave equation on GRUT interior metric
Does NOT assume information saturation implies elasticity (tested from params)
Does NOT assume dissipation disappears at endpoint (memory damping modeled)
Proxy reactive classification is SUPERSEDED — PDE gives mixed_viscoelastic
Classification thresholds are ORDER OF MAGNITUDE conventions
Single-mode oscillator model may miss continuous spectrum features
PDE closure is itself approximate — derived from ODE linearisation, not covariant

transition_width_correction.conclusion: Sharp-boundary impedance is EXCELLENT approximation (< 1% correction)

freeze_note.status: FROZEN — PDE-informed constrained estimate (candidate falsifier channel, weakened but surviving)
freeze_note.revision_reason: PDE closure (Phase III-C) corrected the WP2C proxy eigenfrequency error and reclassified the interior from reactive_candidate to mixed_viscoelastic. The freeze is revised to reflect PDE as the leading classification.
freeze_note.rationale: WP2 was stress-tested through four proxy levels (WP2A-D), then the PDE closure identified a proxy eigenfrequency error (extra 1/R_eq in omega_core). The corrected PDE dispersion relation yields the structural identity omega_0*tau=1 and universal Q=6, reclassifying the BDCC as mixed_viscoelastic. Echo channel weakened from ~3.7% to ~1.1% but not collapsed.
locked_items: Phi-profile as zeroth-order graded transition model
Transition-width corrections small in benchmark regime (< 1%)
Multi-mode corrections negligible under canon dissipation
WP2 mature enough to freeze as candidate falsifier channel
PDE structural identity omega_0*tau=1 (exact within current closure)

superseded_proxy_result.note: Useful historical step. Proxy correctly captured functional form of damping; only the eigenfrequency magnitude was wrong.

active_research_targets: Covariant interior wave equation (beyond ODE linearisation)
Ab initio mode spectrum from metric perturbation theory
Kerr generalization for rotating black holes
Nonlinear mode coupling at large perturbation amplitudes
Independent verification of omega_0*tau=1 via covariant treatment — RESOLVED (covariant_closure confirms)

pde_closure.status: ANALYSIS COMPLETE — approximate, zeroth-order. Covariant closure CONFIRMS structural results.
pde_closure.approach: Linearised perturbation equation from collapse ODE system around BDCC equilibrium

eigenfrequency.correct: omega_0^2 = beta_Q * GM / R_eq^3 (from linearisation of force balance)

structural_identity.proof: omega_0 = sqrt(beta_Q * GM / R_eq^3), tau_local ~ sqrt(R_eq^3 / (2GM)). Product = sqrt(beta_Q/2) = 1.0 for beta_Q = 2.
structural_identity.scope: Exact within the current PDE closure framework (Tier-0 local tau, constrained endpoint law, linearised ODE)
structural_identity.interpretation: The oscillation timescale and local memory-relaxation timescale are locked together. The BDCC always sits at peak of the memory damping function. This naturally favors a mixed viscoelastic regime.
not_claimed_as: A universal law independent of the closure used
Final canon beyond its current framework
Proof of bandwidth saturation in an absolute sense
Valid under alternative tau closures without re-derivation

universal_Q.mass_dependence: None — mass-independent for all astrophysical masses tested (10 to 10^9 M_sun)
universal_Q.note: Consequence of the structural identity. When omega*tau = 1, Q depends only on the ratio of barrier parameters to memory coupling.

pde_closure.impact_on_echo_channel: Echo amplitude reduced from ~3.7% to ~1.1% — candidate falsifier channel weakened but surviving
nonclaims: PDE is derived from ODE linearisation, NOT covariant wave equation
Spatial structure from Regge-Wheeler separation, not GRUT metric
Memory coupling simplified to single tau_eff at equilibrium
Q = 6 (mixed) replaces Q = 515 (reactive) from proxy — proxy is SUPERSEDED
Structural identity omega_0*tau=1 is exact within THIS closure only
Result needs independent verification via covariant treatment
Does not constitute proof of bandwidth saturation
Does not claim mixed_viscoelastic is the final answer — alternative closures may shift Q

covariant_closure.status: FIRST COVARIANT PASS — effective metric ansatz CONFIRMS PDE structural results
covariant_closure.approach: Effective interior metric ansatz with barrier-corrected lapse and memory-dressed radial metric. Perturbation equation in effective Regge-Wheeler form.
covariant_closure.key_finding: k^2 = F_PDE(omega) / c_eff^2. Eigenfrequencies are zeros of F_PDE, independent of c_eff. PDE dispersion relation SURVIVES in covariant framework.
resolved_closures: Covariant form of perturbation equation (effective Regge-Wheeler with memory)
Interior metric ansatz (effective lapse with barrier correction)
How memory enters covariantly (constitutive relation in B_eff)
remaining_closures: Explicit GRUT covariant field equations (fundamental theory)
Propagating memory field equation (memory as tensor field)
Interior tortoise coordinate (full radial metric profile)
Israel junction conditions at transition boundary
nonclaims: Effective metric ansatz is NOT derived from covariant GRUT field equations
Angular structure from Schwarzschild separation, NOT from GRUT metric tensor
Memory enters as local constitutive relation, NOT as propagating field
Eigenfrequencies PRESERVED from PDE (same dispersion relation), NOT rederived
Reflection coefficient APPROXIMATE (metric-corrected impedance, ±20%)
mixed_viscoelastic classification PRESERVED but NOT proven final
Covariant pass does NOT resolve the fundamental GRUT field equations

WP3_shadow_accretion.status: ANALYSIS COMPLETE — null at leading order (conditional on Schwarzschild-like exterior)
WP3_shadow_accretion.question: Could a core at 1/3 r_s affect observable shadow/photon-region/accretion behavior?

result.eddington_luminosity: IDENTICALLY NULL — L_Edd depends only on M (conserved)
result.disk_spectrum: IDENTICALLY NULL — Novikov-Thorne depends on exterior metric only

WP3_shadow_accretion.causal_argument: BDCC at R_eq = r_s/3 is inside the horizon (compactness C=3). All static observables (shadow, photon sphere, ISCO) are determined by the exterior metric at r > r_s. The BDCC has no causal influence on these observables at leading order.

underdetermined.tidal_love_numbers: NOT COMPUTED — potential non-null observable (reflecting boundary at R_eq could break TLN=0)
underdetermined.echo_accretion_coupling: NOT COMPUTED — second-order effect at ~1% echo amplitude level

WP3_shadow_accretion.only_non_null_channel: WP2 echoes (~1.1% amplitude, dynamic observable, not static)
nonclaims: Null results are CONDITIONAL on WP1 Schwarzschild-like exterior
Does NOT prove BDCC is undetectable (WP2 echo channel survives)
Tidal Love numbers NOT computed — remain an open observable
Kerr NOT attempted — all results for non-rotating BHs only
No EHT, LIGO, or detector-level predictions made
Ringdown echo estimates are CONDITIONAL on WP1 exterior assessment and PDE closure model
WP3 shadow/accretion null-result is CONDITIONAL on Schwarzschild-like exterior — if exterior is modified, all null results need re-evaluation
The impedance model is a sharp-boundary approximation; WP2D confirms < 1% transition-width correction
The Boltzmann model (r_amp ≈ 0) remains a viable alternative if the BDCC is dissipative
Echoes are NOT predicted to exist — the module computes what they WOULD look like under assumptions
The pre-PDE proxy result (Q~515, r~0.98, ~3.7% echo) is SUPERSEDED — do not cite as current best estimate
PDE closure is approximate (ODE linearisation) — confirmed by first covariant pass but not the final word on interior response
Structural identity omega_0*tau=1 is exact within the current closure framework — confirmed by covariant ansatz but not proven universal
Covariant closure is an effective metric ansatz, NOT derived from fundamental GRUT field equations
7 missing closures remain (fundamental field equations, propagating memory, Kerr, junction conditions, tidal Love numbers, tortoise coordinate, nonlinear modes)

field_equations.status: FIRST COVARIANT PASS — auxiliary memory field (scalarized first pass)

conservation.status: compatible at the EFFECTIVE LEVEL — not proven from variational principles

conservation.collapse_sector: no separate conservation — energy exchange between matter, memory, barrier

sector_reductions.strong_field: RECOVERED at effective level — matches collapse.py force balance, endpoint law, structural identity

local_tau_hypothesis.equation: tau_eff_local = tau0 * F(rho, C, K, t_dyn)
local_tau_hypothesis.description: Cosmological tau0 is too large for direct reuse in stellar collapse. The local memory timescale must be a derived function of local conditions. Deriving F is the next research objective.
local_tau_hypothesis.note: Phase G showed that the collapse sector is governed by a timescale-competition law, not by a universal fixed-memory constant applied blindly across regimes.

step_budget_diagnostics.description: First-class audit fields. When step_budget_exhausted=true AND t_total_over_t_ff << 1, the result is step-budget-contaminated.

primary_targets.name: H(z)
primary_targets.definition: Emit arrays of (z, H) where z=1/a-1 for a0=1

primary_targets.name: fσ8(z)
primary_targets.definition: Emit arrays of (z, fsigma8, f, sigma8, D_over_D0) from linear growth
